$NVDA is holding around the $200 area, with VWAP near $201 and the rising 200-day moving average around $191.    This puts the $195-$200 zone in focus as the first major dip area of the new range, but it is not confirmed as a final bottom or a fresh breakout zone yet.    Short-term traders may look for bounce opportunities around $195 with strict risk management. On the upside, $208-$212 could become a key resistance area near the 50-day moving average.    The cleaner swing setup may come from a deeper pullback toward the $190-$195 zone rather than chasing every small move around $200.
